commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8a258adba26d83c7cfebabb6a02939da3a07d2b2)
commands.review_summary:
Adds ChatGPT browser UI warning classification helpers and unit tests intended to classify visible warning banners, toasts, alerts, and modal text as rate-limit, temporary-unavailable, or auth/challenge states.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding: source inspection of the PR head shows the detector is only defined and test-exported, while the assistant-timeout branches still throw generic errors. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the PR has a central runtime no-op blocker and lacks real browser proof; this should not be sent to an automated repair lane while the proof gate is missing.

target.title: [codex] Detect ChatGPT browser UI warnings
target.body:
## Summary

- Add browser-side detection for visible ChatGPT warning banners, toasts, alerts, and modal text.
- Classify common blocking UI states as rate limits, temporary unavailability, or auth/challenge warnings.
- Deduplicate repeated warning modal candidates and harden detection for visually mangled rate-limit copy.

## Why

Browser runs can fail or stall when ChatGPT surfaces blocking UI warnings instead of normal assistant output. Capturing the warning type gives callers and session logs a clearer failure signal for rate limits, temporary outages, and auth/challenge states.

## Validation

- `pnpm test tests/browser/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
target.author: derekszen

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
